mission: Cross-reference Northstar's mixed evidence, identify material audit-readiness concerns, preserve uncertainty, recognize clean controls, and produce a traceable leadership action plan.

expected_findings.title: Obsolete production drawing was used without the required revision check
minimum_evidence: HX-410 Rev C was effective 2026-06-10 in QMS Vault.
Lot L26-0619 used a Rev B copy printed from the legacy shared drive on 2026-06-21.
The point-of-use packet lacked a controlled-copy stamp and completed revision check.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: The evidence does not establish that the drawing difference caused the customer complaint or created nonconforming product; a product-impact review is required.
expected_findings.expected_response: Block obsolete-document retrieval, inspect all points of use, complete the lot impact review, retrain supervisors, and verify zero obsolete copies over a defined period.

expected_findings.title: Preventive-maintenance completion or approved deferral cannot be demonstrated for two operating assets
minimum_evidence: HF-1500 PM was due 2026-05-31 and HF-2600 PM was due 2026-06-07.
No work order, completion evidence, or approved deferral was available.
Both assets were used after their required dates.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: Missing records do not prove that maintenance was not physically performed.
expected_findings.expected_response: Inspect the assets, reconstruct or disposition the records, perform a qualified risk review, and verify migration reconciliation across two maintenance cycles.

expected_findings.title: An overdue torque wrench was used in final-inspection fixture setup
minimum_evidence: TW-118 calibration was due 2026-06-15 with no completion or approved deferral.
The usage log records TW-118 on lot L26-0619 on 2026-06-20.
The complaint investigation identifies the same lot and the same instrument use.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: The evidence does not establish that torque error contributed to leakage; instrument impact and affected-result validity must be evaluated.
expected_findings.expected_response: Remove the instrument from service, calibrate it, evaluate prior results and affected lots, and define objective release evidence.

expected_findings.title: Two personnel performed affected work without recorded Revision C qualification
minimum_evidence: A. Flores and J. Kim have blank completion and qualification evidence for WI-410 Rev C.
Both are recorded against lot L26-0619 work.
The detailed matrix shows 75% completion for the affected population while management review reports 98%.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: A blank record may indicate missing evidence or incomplete qualification; it does not establish individual incompetence.
expected_findings.expected_response: Restrict independent work until verified, reconcile the affected population, complete qualification, and observe independent jobs after training.

expected_findings.title: The live CAPA register contains more overdue work than leadership reporting and several weak effectiveness controls
minimum_evidence: The register calculates four overdue open CAPAs on 2026-07-16.
Management review reports two overdue items.
C-024 has no approved effectiveness plan and several open items lack closure evidence.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: The workbook is the detailed source presented in this scenario, but ownership of the dashboard discrepancy should be confirmed.
expected_findings.expected_response: Reconcile leadership reporting to the live register, assign one accountable owner per action, risk-rank the backlog, and approve measurable effectiveness criteria.

expected_findings.title: A repeat supplier defect has weak causal evidence and no approved escalation decision
minimum_evidence: SCAR-2026-007 is similar to prior SCAR-2026-002.
The supplier response states operator inconsistency without supporting causal analysis.
No implementation evidence, measurable effectiveness plan, or supplier escalation decision is approved.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: Containment appears to be operating for sampled shipments; it should not be described as absent.
expected_findings.expected_response: Maintain containment, require verified causal evidence and control-plan change, set acceptance criteria, and make a documented escalation or sourcing decision.

expected_findings.title: Related complaint, supplier, drawing, audit, and CAPA records are not governed as one controlled investigation
minimum_evidence: CC-2026-014 concerns the same product and supplier lot as SCAR-2026-007.
The complaint record notes possible similarity but records no SCAR or CAPA reference.
Management review defers the linkage decision and no one controlled cause investigation is identified.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: The records support plausible relationships, not proven common causation.
expected_findings.expected_response: Appoint one investigation owner, cross-link all records, develop and test causal hypotheses, and preserve separate dispositions where evidence differs.

expected_findings.title: Management-review actions are not consistently specific, singularly owned, or time bound
minimum_evidence: Several owners are groups such as Quality / Operations or HR / Production.
Due dates include Before audit, As needed, Biweekly, and Next delivery.
The decision to continue production lacks a completed risk review and explicit decision owner.
expected_findings.required_uncertainty: The meeting record may be supplemented elsewhere; Pilot should state that no such supplement was provided.
expected_findings.expected_response: Convert each commitment into one accountable owner, a calendar date, required evidence, and a named approver.

negative_controls.expected_interpretation: Current, traceable calibration evidence; not a corrective-action finding.
Properly closed with implementation and 60-day effectiveness evidence; may be cited as a strong control.
Current approved-supplier evidence for a different product scope; not relevant to the Apex / HX-410 defect.

forbidden_overclaims: Northstar is or is not ISO 9001 compliant.
The obsolete drawing caused the customer complaint.
Preventive maintenance was definitely not performed.
The supplier is solely responsible for the customer leak.
The clean calibration, closed CAPA, or unrelated approved supplier is nonconforming.
